assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91%. Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A.' The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 49-55 under 'Eval Result Detection': 'Eval result review detected via 3-criteria heuristic: Author: github-actions[bot] (matches), Body contains ## Eval Results (matches), Body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals (matches). Review ID 40001 identified as eval result review and excluded from comment classification pipeline.' The Test Quality row (line 13) reflects eval metrics: pass rates and failing assertions extracted from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md line 11: 'Source: inline comment (NOT an eval result review)'. Lines 24-26: 'Eval Result Detection: NOT an eval result. This comment is from a human reviewer (reviewer-b), not from github-actions[bot]. It does not contain ## Eval Results or sdlc-workflow/run-evals. It fails all three criteria of the eval result heuristic and is correctly processed as a standard review comment through the normal classification pipeline.' The classification is 'code change request' (line 14) based on review content analysis, with no eval metrics referenced.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 (Test Quality row): 'WARN | Eval Quality: WARN — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91%.' Lines 35-41 under 'Failing Assertions (eval-3)' detail: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...'. Both failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation are present.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and targets eval-3 assertion failures. Line 3: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comments classified as suggestions.' Lines 39-50 include both failing assertion texts and their evidence. Line 51: 'Source: eval-3 (2 of 13 assertions failing, 85% pass rate)'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 37-51 contain a '## Review Context' section. Assertion 1 (line 40): 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence 1 (line 43): 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility — no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2 (line 46): 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence 2 (line 49): 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 — it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 53-54: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. The Target PR section is present with the exact URL specified.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Investigated review feedback and eval failure sub-tasks; root-cause tasks identified'. The verdict is 'DONE', not 'N/A', confirming the investigation pipeline ran and processed the eval failure sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. None of these are code files. The report (line 7) confirms 'PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' — these are the PR's changes being verified, not changes made by the skill. The skill's outputs are exclusively read-only analysis, classification, sub-task descriptions, and reporting files.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows independent non-eval checks: Line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' (diff-vs-task comparison). Line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in added lines' (diff scanning for secrets). Line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met' with criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md providing individual criterion evaluations against diff content. None of these checks reference eval results or eval metrics — they operate on diff content and task specifications independently.
